qas.q: What is the CFETP used for?
qas.a: to plan, develop, manage, and conduct an effective career field training program.

qas.q: Name the four types of training on which CFETP focuses.
qas.a: 1. Initial skills training 2. upgrade training 3. Qualification training 4. Continuation training

qas.q: What type of training does the formal resident course at Keesler AFB provide?
Name the Air Force publication that serves as a training contract between AETC and our 1C5X1 functional managers
What official guide contains an overview of all the different career fields?
What skill level is awarded after the successful completion of tech school?
Once you are qualified in C2BMO duty position and upon completion of your CDC's what skill level are you awarded?
What is the AFSC of a C2BMO journeyman
what is the AFSC of a C2BMO superintendent
what is added to the beginning of an AFSC to identify an ability, skill, special qualification, or system?
What is a three-character code that identifies special experience and training not otherwise identified within the military personnel data system?
An increase in force readiness above that required for normal readiness
A normal state of readiness
The maximum force readiness
An increased intelligence watch and strengthened security measures
An increase in force readiness, but less than maximum readiness
What are the three attack warning conditions?
What warning is used when an attack by hostile aircraft in imminent?
What is the exercise term for air defense warning condition YELLOW?
What is the normal warning condition used when an attack by hostile aircraft, space vehicles, or missiles is no longer considered probable?
Who are the military authorities for the emergency security control of air traffic (ESCAT) plan in the forty-eight contiguous states, including Washington DC and Alaska?
qas.a: The commander NORAD or individual NORAD region/sector commanders

qas.q: Which priority of the EATPL includes the President of the United States and the Prime minister of Canada?
